enonce.texte:
Match the medical terms with their correct definitions.

1. Prescription
2. Medicine
3. Dose
4. Pharmacy
5. Consultation
6. Treatment
Definitions:
A. The amount of medicine to take at one time
B. A written order from a doctor for medicine
C. The care given to treat an illness
D. A shop where medicines are sold
E. A substance used to cure or prevent illness
F. A meeting with a doctor for medical advice

questions.texte: Match each word with the correct definition by writing the correct letter (A-F) in your notebook.

enonce.texte:
Read the prescription and answer the questions.

PRESCRIPTION

Patient: Jean Pierre

Age: 12 years

Medicine: Paracetamol

Dose: 1 tablet every 6 hours

Duration: 3 days

Instructions: Take after meals with water.

Doctor: Dr. Ngono

Date: 15th June 2025

questions.texte: What is the name of the patient?
questions.reponse: The patient's name is Jean Pierre.

questions.texte: What medicine is prescribed?
questions.reponse: The medicine prescribed is Paracetamol.

questions.texte: How often should the patient take the medicine?
questions.reponse: The patient should take 1 tablet every 6 hours.

questions.texte: How long should the patient take this medicine?
questions.reponse: The patient should take it for 3 days.

questions.texte: When should the patient take the medicine?
questions.reponse: The patient should take it after meals with water.

enonce.texte:
Read the medicine label and answer the questions.

MEDICINE LABEL

Name: Cough Syrup

Dosage: 2 teaspoons (10 ml) every 8 hours

For: Cough and sore throat

Warnings:

  • Do not exceed the recommended dose
  • Not for children under 6 years
  • Consult a doctor if symptoms persist
  • Keep out of reach of children

Expiry date: December 2026

questions.texte: What is the name of this medicine?
questions.reponse: The name of the medicine is Cough Syrup.

questions.texte: What is the dosage for this medicine?
questions.reponse: The dosage is 2 teaspoons (10 ml) every 8 hours.

questions.texte: What is this medicine used for?
questions.reponse: This medicine is used for cough and sore throat.

questions.texte: Explain why this medicine is not for children under 6 years.
questions.reponse: This medicine is not for children under 6 years because it may be too strong for them. Children's bodies are smaller and they need different doses. The label warns that it is not safe for young children.

questions.texte: What should you do if the symptoms do not go away?
questions.reponse: If the symptoms do not go away, you should consult a doctor.

enonce.texte:
Read the instructions and answer the questions.

INSTRUCTIONS FOR TAKING MEDICINE

When you take medicine, follow these important rules:

  1. Always follow the doctor's instructions.
  2. Take the medicine at the right time.
  3. Never take more than the recommended dose.
  4. Always take the medicine with water.
  5. Store the medicine in a cool, dry place.
  6. Keep medicine away from children.
  7. Do not share your medicine with others.
  8. Check the expiry date before taking.

questions.texte: List three important rules for taking medicine.
questions.reponse: Three important rules are: follow the doctor's instructions, take the medicine at the right time, and never take more than the recommended dose. (Any three correct rules).

questions.texte: Why should we not share medicine with others?
questions.reponse: We should not share medicine because what works for one person may not be safe for another. Different people need different doses and medicines. Sharing medicine can be dangerous.

questions.texte: What should you check before taking any medicine?
questions.reponse: Before taking any medicine, you should check the expiry date.

questions.texte: Explain why it is important to keep medicine in a cool, dry place.
questions.reponse: Medicine should be kept in a cool, dry place because heat and moisture can damage the medicine. It may become less effective or even harmful.

questions.texte: Why is self-medication dangerous according to these instructions?
questions.reponse: Self-medication is dangerous because you might take the wrong medicine, take the wrong dose, or ignore important warnings. This can make you sicker or cause serious health problems. You should always follow a doctor's instructions.

enonce.texte:
Read the dialogue and answer the questions.

Doctor: Good morning, Sarah. What is the problem?

Sarah: Good morning, Doctor. I have a bad headache and a fever.

Doctor: Let me check your temperature. You have a fever. I will give you some medicine.

Sarah: What should I do?

Doctor: Take this medicine three times a day after meals. Drink plenty of water and rest. If you don't feel better in two days, come back to see me.

Sarah: Thank you, Doctor. I will follow your advice.

questions.texte: What are Sarah's symptoms?
questions.reponse: Sarah has a bad headache and a fever.

questions.texte: What did the doctor tell Sarah to do?
questions.reponse: The doctor told Sarah to take medicine three times a day after meals, drink plenty of water, and rest.

questions.texte: What should Sarah do if she does not feel better in two days?
questions.reponse: If she does not feel better in two days, she should go back to see the doctor.

questions.texte: Why is it important to take medicine after meals?
questions.reponse: It is important to take medicine after meals because some medicines can upset the stomach if taken on an empty stomach. Food helps to protect the stomach and helps the medicine work better.

questions.texte: Why is rest important when you have a fever?
questions.reponse: Rest is important when you have a fever because it helps your body fight the infection. When you rest, your body can use its energy to heal and recover faster.

enonce.texte:
Read the medicine warning labels and answer the questions.

Warning 1:
Do not operate heavy machinery after taking this medicine.
Warning 2:
May cause drowsiness. Avoid driving.
Warning 3:
Do not consume alcohol while taking this medicine.
Warning 4:
Keep out of reach of children.
Warning 5:
Store at room temperature (15-25°C).

questions.texte: What warning should a truck driver be most concerned about?
questions.reponse: A truck driver should be most concerned about Warning 1 and Warning 2: 'Do not operate heavy machinery' and 'May cause drowsiness. Avoid driving.'

questions.texte: According to the warnings, what should you not drink while taking this medicine?
questions.reponse: You should not drink alcohol while taking this medicine.

questions.texte: Why is it important to keep medicine out of reach of children?
questions.reponse: It is important to keep medicine out of reach of children because children might think it is candy and eat it. This can be very dangerous and cause serious health problems.

questions.texte: What might happen if you take this medicine and then drive a car?
questions.reponse: If you take this medicine and then drive, you might feel drowsy and have difficulty concentrating. This could lead to an accident.

questions.texte: Why is it important to store medicine at room temperature?
questions.reponse: Storing medicine at room temperature is important because extreme temperatures (too hot or too cold) can damage the medicine. It might become less effective or even harmful.

enonce.texte:
Write a short paragraph (about 80-100 words) giving advice to a friend who is sick.

Include:

  • What they should do (see a doctor, rest, drink water)
  • What medicine they should take (or avoid)
  • When they should go back to the doctor

questions.texte: Write a paragraph giving advice to a sick friend.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

Dear friend, I am sorry to hear that you are sick. Here is some advice for you. First, you should see a doctor as soon as possible. The doctor will examine you and give you the right medicine. Second, you must rest at home. Your body needs energy to fight the sickness. Third, drink plenty of water and eat healthy food. If the doctor gives you medicine, take it exactly as prescribed. Do not take more than the recommended dose. If you do not feel better after two days, go back to the doctor. Take care of yourself and get well soon.

enonce.texte:
Read the prescription and answer the questions.

PRESCRIPTION

Patient: Marie-Claire

Age: 10 years

Date: 20th June 2025

Medicine: Amoxicillin (antibiotic)

Dose: 250 mg three times a day

Duration: 7 days

Instructions: Take with food. Complete the full course even if you feel better.

Side effects: May cause stomach upset. If you have a rash, stop and see your doctor.

Doctor: Dr. Mbock

questions.texte: What medicine has been prescribed for Marie-Claire?
questions.reponse: Marie-Claire has been prescribed Amoxicillin (an antibiotic).

questions.texte: How much should Marie-Claire take each day?
questions.reponse: Marie-Claire should take 250 mg three times a day.

questions.texte: Why should Marie-Claire take this medicine with food?
questions.reponse: She should take it with food because the medicine may cause stomach upset. Food helps to protect the stomach.

questions.texte: What should Marie-Claire do if she gets a rash?
questions.reponse: If she gets a rash, she should stop taking the medicine and see her doctor. A rash could mean she is allergic to the medicine.

questions.texte: Why is it important to complete the full course of antibiotics even if you feel better?
questions.reponse: It is important to complete the full course of antibiotics even if you feel better because some bacteria may still be in your body. If you stop early, the bacteria could come back and become resistant to the medicine. This makes the infection harder to treat next time.

enonce.texte:
⚠️ The Dangers of Self-Medication

Self-medication means taking medicine without a doctor's prescription. This is dangerous for several reasons:

  • You might take the wrong medicine for your illness.
  • You might take the wrong dose - too much or too little.
  • You might have an allergic reaction.
  • You might delay proper treatment.
  • You might have side effects that you don't know about.

Always consult a doctor before taking any medicine.

questions.texte: What is self-medication?
questions.reponse: Self-medication means taking medicine without a doctor's prescription.

questions.texte: List three dangers of self-medication.
questions.reponse: Three dangers of self-medication are: taking the wrong medicine, taking the wrong dose, and having an allergic reaction. (Any three correct answers).

questions.texte: Why might self-medication delay proper treatment?
questions.reponse: Self-medication might delay proper treatment because if you take the wrong medicine, your illness might get worse while you are taking the wrong treatment. This means the real problem is not being treated correctly.

questions.texte: Explain why it is important to consult a doctor before taking medicine.
questions.reponse: It is important to consult a doctor before taking medicine because doctors know which medicine is right for your illness. They know the correct dose and can check if you have any allergies. This helps to prevent dangerous side effects and ensures you get the right treatment.

questions.texte: What advice would you give to someone who wants to take medicine without seeing a doctor?
questions.reponse: My advice would be: Please do not take medicine without seeing a doctor. It is dangerous and can cause serious health problems. Only a doctor can tell you the right medicine and dose for your illness. If you cannot see a doctor, go to a pharmacy and ask a pharmacist for advice. Never take medicine that was prescribed for someone else.

intitule: Writing: A Dialogue with a Pharmacist

enonce.texte:
Write a short dialogue (about 8-10 exchanges) between a patient and a pharmacist.

The patient has a headache and wants to buy some medicine. The pharmacist gives advice.

Include:

  • Greetings
  • Asking about the problem
  • Giving advice about medicine
  • Instructions on how to take the medicine
  • Warnings

questions.texte: Write a dialogue between a patient and a pharmacist.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

Patient: Good morning! I have a headache. Do you have any medicine?

Pharmacist: Good morning. How long have you had the headache?

Patient: I have had it since yesterday.

Pharmacist: Do you have any other symptoms?

Patient: No, just the headache.

Pharmacist: I recommend this painkiller. Take one tablet every 6 hours.

Patient: How should I take it?

Pharmacist: Take it with water after a meal. Do not take more than 3 tablets in 24 hours.

Patient: Are there any side effects?

Pharmacist: It may cause drowsiness. Do not drive after taking it. If the pain does not go away in 2 days, see a doctor.

Patient: Thank you very much!

Pharmacist: You're welcome. Take care and get well soon.
